video_title: Top Pork Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Rob's Sausage Rolls
restaurants.guy_intro: Pork and fennel sausage roll Rob's pork and fennel sausage roll is the best I've ever had it just melts in your mouth.

dishes.description: Pork shoulder ground with mirepoix and fresh fennel, mixed with sourdough breadcrumbs, wrapped in thousand-layer puff pastry, baked until golden.
dishes.guy_response: That's not a sausage sandwich. No, it's not a pig in a blanket. It is its own entity and it is dynamite. Thank you. The pork and fennel sausage roll it's moist and the fennel is perfect.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Big Pork Chop)
restaurants.guy_intro: That's a let that can come. It's like the Flintstone brontosaurus type pork chop.

dishes.description: Large pork chop, brined overnight, seasoned with salt, oregano, garlic salt, sofrito, and annatto, then baked and fried to order. Served with white rice, beans, sweet plantains, and chimichurri.
dishes.guy_response: Well, I'll tell you this, right off the bat, that's dangerous. Chimichurri is dynamite. The presentation, of course, is amazing. The meat on the bone, surrounded by the fat. Mm. When you can get a bite of the pork chop and the chicharron all together, that's prime time. Mm. But I don't even know why you go to all that effort to serve that pork when the rice and beans are money. This just wrecks rice and beans for every other culture. Mm. I eat that every day.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Korean Bulgogi)
restaurants.guy_intro: Playing the spicy pork bulgogi. So today, I got the spicy pork bulgogi.

dishes.description: Thinly sliced pork shoulder marinated in a blend of sweet onions, pineapple, ginger, green onion, garlic, Korean chili powder, rice cooking wine, soy, gochujang paste, sesame seeds, and sesame oil, then sautéed. Served with pickled carrot slaw, white rice, and romaine lettuce for wrapping.
dishes.guy_response: This has got a good Korean Nashville barbecue. Cross play in there to it. Delicious pickled veg. A great way to try Korean food. You could tell I didn't like it, by the way. I just powered through the entire lettuce leaf, stacked up with half of the dish. And I like it. The bulgogi is so tender. Zesty but sweet at the same time. That slaw with it. Take me to Flavortown, son.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Italian Ravioli)
restaurants.guy_intro: We use one third, one third, one third. Beef, veal, and pork. Nice and hot. Really break this meat apart. We don't want any big chunks.

dishes.description: Handmade pasta filled with a blend of beef, veal, pork, shallots, garlic, and spices, combined with a ricotta, mozzarella, Romano cheese, and parsley filling. Served with a simple San Marzano marinara sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. That is all thriller, no filler. You got the balance. Exact amount of creaminess. You taste the texture of the ricotta still. Then you get the texture of the meat. And the flavor's right on point with it. The marinara adds that acid. A little bit of that sting from some of that cheese right on top of it. Here, you want to try some?

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Vietnamese Banh Mi)
restaurants.guy_intro: Pork banh mi. Let's go, guys. Banh mi is one of my favorites.

dishes.description: A seven-inch baguette hollowed out and filled with garlic mayo, house-made chicken liver pate, head cheese (made from pig face, neck, and ham), barbecue pork, Vietnamese bologna (jalua), pickled carrots and daikon, cucumbers, jalapenos, and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: I mean, there's just so many different components. The balance is great. As you chew it, you just get, oh, there it is. Oh, now I got some sour. Now I got some heat. Now I got a little jalapeno. Now I got the freshness of the cucumber. It just keeps trumping itself as you enjoy it. Exactly. I dig it. Thank you. The inside is so spongy. It really absorbs the flavor of the meat. The pickled daikon and carrots gives it a good crunch.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Scallops & Pork Belly)
restaurants.guy_intro: Scallops and belly up in the window. This dish is to die for. Scallops melt in your mouth. And then the cherry on top of the sundae is a pork belly, which is so rich in flavor.

dishes.description: Pork belly braised for hours with onions, carrots, celery, garlic, thyme, and apple cider, then seared. Scallops seasoned and seared. Served with parsnip puree, beets, snap peas, Brussels sprouts, green curly kale, waffle fries, and a butter-thickened jus.
dishes.guy_response: That's a really good scallop, man. You cooked it perfectly. Super tender. There's great crust. The jus is a really nice complement to it. That parsnip puree, I'm out of time. The time that you all are taking here to braise the belly and to serve it up with the seasonal veg carries it through. Nice job, man. Really nice job. Scallops going in. Just lovely and tender and moist and succulent. The pork belly, it's a little bit of apple cider, gives it that nice crispiness and that subtle dry flavor.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Pork Dumplings)
restaurants.guy_intro: Pork dumplings going to 75. The pork dumplings, amazing, delicious, nice little spicy component. You have to get them every time you come.

dishes.description: Ground pork butt and fat back mixed with apples, onions, ginger, tamari, sesame oil, and spices, wrapped in homemade dough, boiled, then pan-seared for a crispy bottom. Served with multiple dipping sauces including a vinegar-based tare, fragrant red chili oil, and chili crisp.
dishes.guy_response: What? It's umami-bami dynamite. It's rich. Chili crisp is a perfect perfect. You need the Fresno because there's so much boom, boom, boom going on. The oil is a nice little spice note. Just in the name of science, I normally don't like to have three dumplings in a minute. But for you guys, I'm doing this. Like my dad used to say, it's not if I want more tacos, it's do I need more tacos. I understand now, Dad. What's up, y'all? I have some pork dumplings. The pork is absolutely delicious. You get the bit of the crisp from the searing. Total flavor explosion.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Korean Tacos)
restaurants.guy_intro: Two more Korean pork tacos going down. The Korean pork tacos are amazing. Very unique flavor to Kansas City. Spicy, sweet. I love the kimchi over the tacos. That's kind of a nice little treat.

dishes.description: Pork loin slathered overnight in a marinade of garlic, ginger, soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, sesame oil, Korean chili powder, and sugar, then cooked. Served in tortillas with homemade kimchi, Baja slaw, ginger-infused hoisin drizzle, cilantro, and toasted sesame seeds.
dishes.guy_response: That's a good bite. It's good from here. Thank you very much, camera. It's really good. The pork's a nice play. It's a good amount of spice. Kimchi's got the funk. Got a little bit of heat. Korean is what you're trying to invoke. You did it. Right on.

restaurants.name: Brian's Biscuits & Gravy
restaurants.guy_intro: Signature biscuits and sausage gravy. So who taught you how to make biscuits and gravy? My mom.

dishes.description: Homemade biscuits made with flour, baking powder, and milk, baked until fluffy. Served with a thick, rich sausage gravy made from browned sausage, salt, pepper, and milk.
dishes.guy_response: That's a real deal biscuit right there.

restaurants.name: Nikki's Pad Thai
restaurants.guy_intro: Pad Thai pork, please. All right, Nikki, what are we going to make first?

dishes.description: Rice noodles stir-fried in a hot wok with oil, sweet radish, garlic, and pork, then tossed with a homemade tamarind sauce, broth, and chili. Finished with beansprouts, onions, and peanuts.
dishes.guy_response: Too spicy? For me? Yeah. Oh. The tamarind sauce. Yes. That's really what gets the whole party started. Little sweet there, crunchy peanut. You get a touch of the chili flake coming through. I love that the noodles are really al dente. It's authentic. You make it just like you make for your family? It's my family like this, and my mom cook like this. That's it. That's fun. You're fun, Nicky. Huh? I tell you what, no deal. I have a pad thai right here. Delicious. It's rich. It's got great meat and spice flavors to it.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Pork Chiseling Sandwich)
restaurants.guy_intro: Pork chiseling sandwich in the window. What are we doing first? Our barbecue base.

dishes.description: Cubed pork fried until crispy, then tossed on the flat top with a sweet barbecue sauce and Swiss cheese. Served on a toasted bun with coleslaw and crispy onion strings.
dishes.guy_response: That lights out, bro. It's all the flavors of a great pork barbecue sandwich, but with just texture after texture after texture. The entire piece of pork is caramelized. You were talking about that the cut was critical to make sure that you have the right ratio between how crispy it's going to be on the outside and how tender and juicy it'll be on the inside. And it's the perfect balance of it. The sweetness is there, but it's not overly sweet. One element takes over the top. To me, the Swiss cheese. There's a bit of funk that's in the back of it that ties it all together. You're not a fan of Chisley. This is like gateway Chisley.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Crunch Burger)
restaurants.guy_intro: Crunch burger. I've never had anything like it before. So what makes it crunch? Pork rinds.

dishes.description: Two beef patties seasoned and smashed, topped with spicy barbecue sauce and cheddar cheese. Layered with fried pork rinds (chicharrones) and Juke sauce on a bun with fresh produce.
dishes.guy_response: Smash. That chicharron in there. Juke sauce is great, too. Mm. Burgers cook perfectly. Nice melt on the cheese. You got all the produce. You need in there. But it's when you get that extra little crunch out of that chicharron, mm. Dynamite burger. Thank you. Cooked right, seasoned right, enough ingredients, ridiculously too big, and the chicharron made it. Thank you. Thank you much.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Pork Tamales)
restaurants.guy_intro: World champion. Pork tamale ready? It's the best. We're going to make tamales. Tamales. I'm going to do the pork. Pork tamale.

dishes.description: Pork butt cooked with salt, onions, Mexican oregano, garlic, and bay leaves. A sauce is made from guajillo, California, and chili de arbor peppers. Masa dough is mixed with salt, baking soda, shortening, pork stock, and the chili sauce. The pork and masa are wrapped in corn husks and steamed.
dishes.guy_response: They're delicious. The masa has great texture. The pork is delicious. Not too spicy. And you got a good ratio. It's not a bunch of masa and a little bit of pork inside. This is like a nice 50-50.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Tres Hombres BBQ)
restaurants.guy_intro: Tres hombre sandwich. We're going to build our dry rub for our pulled pork.

dishes.description: A sandwich featuring pulled pork (Boston butt with dry rub and mustard/pickle brine slather, smoked 12 hours) and beef brisket (with dry rub and mustard/pickle brine slather, smoked 16-18 hours). Layered with Albano fontina cheese, house thick barbecue sauce, and pickled red onions on a bun.
dishes.guy_response: This is a monster. Delicious brisket. Love the sausage. Pulled pork's nice and tender. It's legit. It's real deal. It's great flavor. Well done, chef. Thank you. Tres hombres. You have to really press it down to fit in your mouth. The brisket is very tender. It's really delicious.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (BBQ Mac & Cheese)
restaurants.guy_intro: Pork or mac in the window. The barbecue pork, mac, and cheese all around. Just a very flavorful dish. Oh, I think it's kind of a guilty pleasure, actually.

dishes.description: Pulled pork seasoned with a complex spice rub, grilled with mustard, and braised in beer, cola, and cider vinegar. Served over macaroni and cheese made with multiple cheeses (American, cheddar, Gouda, cream cheese) and a buffalo sauce mixture. Topped with a Dr. Pepper-based barbecue sauce and pickled jalapenos.
dishes.guy_response: It's a dynamite dish. Everything's got to be right. Any one part of this not being right, you just put two sides together and call it a main dish. That's right on top of right and added some more right. The pork is delicious and not dried out. The barbecue sauce has got fantastic tang and not overly sweet. The mac sauce gives you that thin, silky, creamy texture. And the real kicker to the whole thing is the diced jalapenos. A little bit of the acidity, a little bit of the heat. Delicious dish. I mean, like, really have to take it away from me. I want to eat it all kind of dish.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Dad's Burger)
restaurants.guy_intro: Best burger right now. Let's do the dad's burger.

dishes.description: An eight-ounce beef patty with chopped bacon crusted onto one side and blue cheese on the other, cooked on a flat top. Served on a hamburger bun with a homemade Aleppo chili spread and fries.
dishes.guy_response: Ladies and gentlemen, this is the bounce. And this is out of bounds. This thing is righteous. Thank you. Dude. Take a look here at how the bacon and the cheese have just melded. Killer burger. This is a cross between hoedads and squeeze-in. I mean, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is, this is! And this is, this is up there. That level of, of burger greatness. Something so simple. Done so right. Grand slam. Awesome. That's movement. It's just delicious every time I come here.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Bacon BLT)
restaurants.guy_intro: What are we going to make? Bacon. All right, man. Let's get into it.

dishes.description: A BLT featuring a nine-ounce patty made from house-cured and applewood-smoked bacon, seasoned and cooked in a wood-fired oven. Served on homemade potato bread with mayonnaise, heirloom tomatoes, organic greens, and a side of house-made onion rings.
dishes.guy_response: Bro, this is what everybody wants a BLT to be like. You're getting the volume of a burger. The flavor of the bacon, the whole top of that patty is just all that crispy, crunchy, unctuous flavor. Then on the inside, it's super tender. It's just amazing. Just done perfectly. I mean, it's BLT of another level. You are a dangerous man. Thank you very much.

restaurants.name: Dolce and Clemente
restaurants.guy_intro: One prosciutto bread, please. Stuffed prosciutto bread. My most favorite thing that Dolce and Clemente serves. Filled with all different varieties of meat.

dishes.description: Homemade high-gluten dough filled with layers of prosciutto, ham, ground mortadella, super salvo, pepperoni, provolone cheese, and crushed black pepper, then baked.
dishes.guy_response: That is ridiculous. But when you cook these Italian meats, and you let all the fat cook into the dough, you just get all the flavor. The depth of flavor in this is massive. The key, though, is how you cook the meat on the outside of the bread. Kisses that crunch. Out of bounds. Seriously, out of bounds. Thank you. Oh, you want this for sure. Trust me. One more bite just for you.

restaurants.name: Fox Brothers
restaurants.guy_intro: Fox Brothers burger up in the window. The Fox Brothers burger is a mouthful. It's sort of a misnomer to call it a burger. It's not a burger at all. It's brisket.

dishes.description: Certified Angus beef brisket trimmed, seasoned with salt and pepper, and smoked overnight for 10-12 hours. The smoked brisket is chopped and formed into a patty, topped with house-made jalapeno mayo and pimento cheese, then broiled. Served on a toasted bun with peppered bacon, pickles, tomato, and onion.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, that's a great burger. You're just, like, cleaning up over there like a lion that just ate a gazelle. I don't know what happened. I went after mine. It's super tender brisket. When you chop that brisket up, you get a chance to incorporate that fat. Yeah. So it makes it even a little bit more unctuous. And adding the pimento cheese and adding the veggies on top of it brings that burger element to it. Mm. That's Fox Brothers right there. Damn, man. Come on. All right, that's full speed, man. That's all-time. All right. No throttle, no bottle, man. That is just getting it done.

restaurants.name: Unknown Restaurant (Mascheroni Pizza)
restaurants.guy_intro: Here's your maescheroni. Just kind of like a meat overload. Da-na-na-na-na-na. Is that where it's from? Yeah. How are we going to start the dough?

dishes.description: Pizza made with a 48-hour fermented dough, topped with house-made red sauce, Wisconsin mozzarella, house-made fennel sausage (Berkshire pork, toasted fennel seeds, garlic, chili flake), and natural casing pepperoni. Baked in a 550-600 degree wood-fired oven.
dishes.guy_response: We got this heirloom pork with the real fat in it and the real flavor, and the fennel is rocking this. A lot of people say, oh, sausage and pepperoni. I've had that all the time. This is way beyond your everyday pizza joint on the corner, sausage and pepperoni. You got to try this one. It's the most heightened, delicious pepperoni sausage pizza you've ever had. You're like, this is what pepperoni is supposed to taste like.
